questions.group_passage:
Green Life Club

Last month, our school started a Green Life Club. I decided to join it because I care about the environment deeply.

One day, our teacher told us about a big problem. Our school produces a lot of waste every day, (8)______ most of it can be recycled. We decided to start a recycling program to help solve this problem.

First, we put special colored bins in every classroom. We also made colorful posters to (9)______ students how to sort their waste correctly. Some students were confused at first, but we patiently helped them understand the importance of recycling.

Two weeks later, we (10)______ the results of our program. We were excited to find that 60% of the waste was now being recycled! Everyone felt proud of our work.

This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)______. I hope more students will join us next semester to make our school (12)______.

One day, our teacher told us about a big problem. Our school produces a lot of waste every day, (8)______ most of it can be recycled. We decided to start a recycling program to help solve this problem.

First, we put special colored bins in every classroom. We also made colorful posters to (9)______ students how to sort their waste correctly. Some students were confused at first, but we patiently helped them understand the importance of recycling.

Two weeks later, we (10)______ the results of our program. We were excited to find that 60% of the waste was now being recycled! Everyone felt proud of our work.

This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)______. I hope more students will join us next semester to make our school (12)______.

questions.group_passage:
Vertical Gardens: Green Walls for Cities

In many big cities, finding green space is difficult. However, some creative architects are solving this problem by building vertical gardens芒聙聰gardens that grow on the walls of buildings.

One famous example is in Milan, Italy. Two residential towers called 'Bosco Verticale' (Vertical Forest) are covered with thousands of trees and plants. These plants don't just look beautiful. They also help reduce air pollution by absorbing CO2 and producing oxygen. Additionally, they provide homes for birds and insects in the city.

Some people worry that these gardens are expensive to maintain. Others think the benefits are worth the cost. 'Plants make people feel happier and healthier,' says one architect. 'That's priceless.'

Many other cities, including Singapore and Shanghai, are now building similar projects. These vertical forests show us that nature and cities can coexist harmoniously.

questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Activities

Many middle school students find it hard to balance schoolwork and extracurricular activities. If you are struggling with this, here are some useful tips to help you.

(17)______ Make a schedule for your week. Write down all your classes, homework, and activities. This way, you can see exactly how much free time you have and plan accordingly.

(18)______ Don't spend too much time on just one thing. If you focus only on studying, you will feel stressed and tired. If you only do activities, your grades may drop. Try to give proper time to both.

(19)______ Use the time between classes or during lunch to review notes or read books. Small moments of study can add up to big results over time.

(20)______ Tell your teachers, parents, or friends if you are feeling overwhelmed. They can offer good advice or help you adjust your schedule to make life easier.

options: A. However, don't forget to relax sometimes.
B. First, plan your time carefully.
C. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
D. On the other hand, keep a balance between work and play.
E. Finally, don't be afraid to ask for help.

questions.label: 莽聼颅忙聳聡氓隆芦莽漏潞
questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Activities

Many middle school students find it hard to balance schoolwork and extracurricular activities. If you are struggling with this, here are some useful tips to help you.

(17)______ Make a schedule for your week. Write down all your classes, homework, and activities. This way, you can see exactly how much free time you have and plan accordingly.

(18)______ Don't spend too much time on just one thing. If you focus only on studying, you will feel stressed and tired. If you only do activities, your grades may drop. Try to give proper time to both.

(19)______ Use the time between classes or during lunch to review notes or read books. Small moments of study can add up to big results over time.

(20)______ Tell your teachers, parents, or friends if you are feeling overwhelmed. They can offer good advice or help you adjust your schedule to make life easier.

options: A. However, don't forget to relax sometimes.
B. First, plan your time carefully.
C. In addition, use your spare time wisely.
D. On the other hand, keep a balance between work and play.
E. Finally, don't be afraid to ask for help.
